reasoning: The informal statement is an "if and only if" (biconditional): AX = B has a solution ⟺ r(A) = r(A, B). The Lean signature uses a one-way implication `→`, formalizing only the forward direction: if a solution exists, then rank A = rank (fromColumns A B). The converse direction (the substantive part — rank equality implies existence of a solution) is omitted entirely. The types and concepts used (Matrix.rank as defined in Mathlib/Data/Matrix/Rank.lean, and fromColumns as the column-concatenation encoding the augmented matrix [A | B] from Mathlib/Data/Matrix/ColumnRowPartitioned.lean) correctly match the informal content, but the overall logical form is a strict weakening of an iff to a single implication. Hence the Lean statement is logically implied by the informal claim but does not imply it.
